behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failures usually happen at the CLI↔git boundary: rename detection depends on actual git diff/status semantics and thresholds, plus how the CLI parses that output. A pure unit test of categorization logic can pass while real git reports a rename differently (or not at all), causing double-counting. Higher levels were considered and rejected: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text contains no AI/ML decision step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report shows exactly one rename and zero added/deleted entries for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary is OS/filesystem + real git diff behavior for binary blobs. In-process fakes often miss real byte handling, encoding markers, and subprocess output nuances that trigger crashes. Higher levels rejected: no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI subprocess, assert exit code is success, no traceback, and binary change is counted in the structured output.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The bug appears when git commands are executed against a repo with unborn HEAD; this is a real subprocess/repo-state interaction. Mocking git responses can hide the exact failure mode (exit code/stdout/stderr behavior). Higher levels rejected: behavior does not involve model output or agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert successful execution with an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on end-to-end interpretation of real repository history (commits, renames, deletions, modifications) through git and CLI parsing/output. Module-only tests can validate arithmetic but still miss real-history mismatches from command invocation or diff-mode assumptions. Higher levels rejected: no AI/ML dependency, no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known numbers of add/delete/rename/modify events across commits, run the CLI subprocess for the target range, and assert each category count matches the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is specifically about git index vs working-tree semantics, which are real repo states exposed via real git plumbing/porcelain. In-process stubs can force equality and miss state-specific discrepancies. Higher levels rejected: no AI/ML decision quality and no chained agentic steps in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run the CLI subprocess in both states, and assert the structured outputs are identical.
